skill.name: vercel-react-view-transitions
skill.description: Guide for implementing smooth, native-feeling animations using React's View Transition API (`<ViewTransition>` component, `addTransitionType`, and CSS view transition pseudo-elements). Use this skill whenever the user wants to add page transitions, animate route changes, create shared element animations, animate enter/exit of components, animate list reorder, implement directional (forward/back) navigation animations, or integrate view transitions in Next.js. Also use when the user mentions view transitions, `startViewTransition`, `ViewTransition`, transition types, or asks about animating between UI states in React without third-party animation libraries.
skill.summary: Implement smooth browser-native animations using React View Transitions API with CSS control
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is legitimate documentation from Vercel Labs for implementing React View Transitions. No malicious code patterns exist. All 781 flagged instances are benign keyword matches in markdown documentation (backticks for code formatting, words like 'keystroke' in UI context, TypeScript type names, and standard documentation URLs).

content.user_title: Add Native View Transitions to React Apps
content.value_statement: Adding smooth page transitions and shared element animations to React apps requires understanding the View Transitions API and its integration patterns. This skill provides step-by-step guidance, CSS recipes, and Next.js patterns from Vercel Labs.
actual_capabilities: Implement shared element transitions between routes
Handle Suspense reveals with proper animations
Integrate view transitions in Next.js App Router
limitations: Requires React canary (not stable React yet)
Browser support limited to Chromium 111+, Firefox 144+, Safari 18.2+
Cannot animate with browser back/forward buttons directly
Nested view transitions have limited enter/exit control

use_cases.title: Add Page Transitions to Next.js App
use_cases.description: Implement smooth slide transitions when users navigate between routes in a Next.js application using the App Router and experimental viewTransition flag.

use_cases.title: Create Shared Element Animations
use_cases.description: Make product images morph smoothly from a grid card to a detail page view, creating a premium feel like native mobile apps.

use_cases.title: Animate Data Loading States
use_cases.description: Replace jarring content flashes with smooth Suspense reveals that gracefully animate content appearing after data fetches.

prompt_templates.title: Add page transitions
prompt_templates.prompt: Add view transitions to my Next.js app so pages slide in when navigating

prompt_templates.title: Implement shared element
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a product grid. When clicking a product, the image should animate from the grid to the detail page hero

prompt_templates.title: Fix animation conflicts
prompt_templates.prompt: My view transitions are breaking when Suspense resolves. How do I prevent nested animations from interfering with page transitions?

prompt_templates.title: Directional navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement forward and back navigation animations that slide in opposite directions so users feel spatial depth in my app

output_examples.input: Add transitions to my product list
output_examples.output: Wrap your product items in <ViewTransition> with unique keys, add CSS for ::view-transition-old/new pseudo-elements, and use startTransition to trigger animations when state changes.

output_examples.input: Why is browser back button not animating?
output_examples.output: The browser back/forward buttons use synchronous popstate events, which are incompatible with startViewTransition. Use router.push() with explicit URLs instead to maintain animation support.

output_examples.input: How do I animate list reordering?
output_examples.output: Assign unique view-transition-name values to each item using their ID. When items reorder, the browser automatically captures the position change and animates it smoothly.

best_practices: Start with the audit step - identify all places needing transitions before writing code
Copy CSS recipes from the skill rather than writing custom animations from scratch
Always test with reduced-motion preferences and graceful degradation for older browsers
anti_patterns: Do not call document.startViewTransition() directly - let the ViewTransition component handle it
Avoid directional slides for lateral navigation (tabs) - this falsely implies spatial depth
Do not wrap ViewTransition inside a div - the component must be the outer wrapper to enable enter/exit

faq.question: Which React version do I need?
faq.answer: React canary is required. Next.js App Router includes it automatically. For other frameworks, install react@canary and react-dom@canary.

faq.question: Does this work with the App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. Enable experimental.viewTransition in next.config.js, use the transitionTypes prop on next/link, and follow the patterns in references/nextjs.md.

faq.question: Why are my animations not working?
faq.answer: Check that ViewTransition wraps the component as the outer element, use startTransition/useDeferredValue/Suspense as triggers, and set default="none" unless you want cross-fade on every change.

faq.question: How do I handle browser support?
faq.answer: Unsupported browsers skip animations gracefully. Always add reduced-motion media query CSS from the css-recipes reference file.

faq.question: Can I animate the browser back button?
faq.answer: No. The browser back/forward buttons use synchronous popstate which is incompatible with startViewTransition. Use router.push() with explicit URLs instead.

faq.question: How do I prevent animations during background updates?
faq.answer: Set default="none" on ViewTransition components. This disables animations unless explicitly enabled with enter/exit/share props.
